key_insight: Organic semiconductor device performance is strongly shaped by weak intermolecular forces and interface-driven supramolecular order, which can be used to tune electronic behavior.
engineering_relevance: Understanding and controlling interfaces offers a practical route to improve organic transistors, LEDs, and photovoltaic devices by making structure-property relationships more predictable.
method: Review recent studies on supramolecular assembly at organic-semiconductor interfaces
Connect molecular orientation and translational symmetry breaking to electronic properties
Summarize progress and challenges in computer simulations of interfacial assembly
evidence_signal: The abstract is a review of recent literature and simulation progress, synthesizing reported advances across transistors, light-emitting diodes, and photovoltaics rather than presenting new experimental data.
watch_item: Performance gains may be difficult to generalize because weak non-covalent interactions create structural disorder that is highly sensitive to processing and interface conditions.
paper_title: The interplay of interfaces, supramolecular assembly, and electronics in organic semiconductors
